npcs.name: Commander Aldric Vane
npcs.role: Grace-Sworn — military leader of the compact
npcs.description: Thirty years in [[The Grace-Sworn]]. He has kept Hartstone neutral through three crises and does not intend this to be the fourth. He is building options. Some of them are things he can never tell [[The Compact Guild|the Guild]] about.

log.note: Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (rival): [[House Vespar]]'s delegation arrives at Hartstone's north gate with forty armed guards. [[Lady Ivra Malt]] presents papers claiming historical title. Impeccably polite, completely immovable.

[[Commander Aldric Vane]] reads [[Lady Ivra Malt]]'s papers carefully, thanks her, and invites the delegation to guest quarters without confirming the claim. He sends a rider to [[Brother Edmar]] before dinner.

Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (rival): [[Lady Ivra Malt]] presents her second claimant's documentation at a formal hearing: Lord Eddan Vespar, third cousin, with a direct treaty right to Hartstone under the Compact of 1187. The records are genuine. [[Emissary Dax Fell]] has a counter.

[[Commander Aldric Vane]] adjourns the hearing. Asks [[Brother Edmar]] to retrieve the 1187 treaty from the archive. Sends a second rider, privately, to someone outside the city. Does not explain who.

[[Commander Aldric Vane]] invokes the armed envoy clause: both delegations must reduce guards to twelve within forty-eight hours. [[Lady Ivra Malt]] protests. [[Emissary Dax Fell]] immediately complies and offers his excess guards to help patrol the tunnels.

npcs.name: Lady Ivra Malt
npcs.role: House Vespar — envoy pressing the northern claim
npcs.description: She arrived with twelve lawyers and forty guards 'for her protection.' She is charming, specific, and never wrong about anything she can cite in writing. She wants Hartstone. She has a fallback position she has not yet revealed. She knows [[Commander Aldric Vane]] better than he would prefer.

log.note: [[House Vespar]]'s delegation arrives at Hartstone's north gate with forty armed guards. [[Lady Ivra Malt]] presents papers claiming historical title. Impeccably polite, completely immovable.

[[Lady Ivra Malt]] presents her second claimant's documentation at a formal hearing: Lord Eddan Vespar, third cousin, with a direct treaty right to Hartstone under the Compact of 1187. The records are genuine. [[Emissary Dax Fell]] has a counter.

Consequence of Emissary Dax Fell (rival): [[Emissary Dax Fell]] produces a ratified [[The Kelrath Envoys|Kelrath]] Council resolution: Hartstone's neutrality supersedes any historical treaty claim. It is dated six months ago. He has been planning this longer than that.

Consequence of Commander Aldric Vane (rival): [[Commander Aldric Vane]] invokes the armed envoy clause: both delegations must reduce guards to twelve within forty-eight hours. [[Lady Ivra Malt]] protests. [[Emissary Dax Fell]] immediately complies and offers his excess guards to help patrol the tunnels.

npcs.name: Guildmaster Sena Cord
npcs.role: Compact Guild — controls trade and tariff income
npcs.description: [[The Compact Guild|The Guild]] has financed [[The Grace-Sworn]] for two generations. Guildmaster Sena Cord knows this. She is not threatening — she is ensuring everyone understands the math. She will back whoever keeps the trade routes open. She has a price. She has not named it yet.

log.note: [[Guildmaster Sena Cord]] requests a private meeting. She confirms [[The Compact Guild]] is aware of the weapons movement. She says: 'The compact pays my tariffs. I would prefer it to continue.' She does not say what she knows about the source.

Consequence of Emissary Dax Fell (ally): [[Emissary Dax Fell]] produces a ratified [[The Kelrath Envoys|Kelrath]] Council resolution: Hartstone's neutrality supersedes any historical treaty claim. It is dated six months ago. He has been planning this longer than that.

Consequence of Commander Aldric Vane (ally): [[Commander Aldric Vane]] invokes the armed envoy clause: both delegations must reduce guards to twelve within forty-eight hours. [[Lady Ivra Malt]] protests. [[Emissary Dax Fell]] immediately complies and offers his excess guards to help patrol the tunnels.

npcs.name: Emissary Dax Fell
npcs.description: He has been in Hartstone for six months before [[House Vespar]] arrived. He knows where every magistrate eats dinner and which ones have debts. He presents as reasonable and collaborative. He is. He is also winning.

log.note: Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (rival): [[House Vespar]]'s delegation arrives at Hartstone's north gate with forty armed guards. [[Lady Ivra Malt]] presents papers claiming historical title. Impeccably polite, completely immovable.
[[Emissary Dax Fell]] dines with [[The Compact Guild]]'s senior merchants the same evening the [[House Vespar]] delegation arrives. He is already here. He has been here six months.

Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (rival): [[Lady Ivra Malt]] presents her second claimant's documentation at a formal hearing: Lord Eddan Vespar, third cousin, with a direct treaty right to Hartstone under the Compact of 1187. The records are genuine. [[Emissary Dax Fell]] has a counter.

[[Emissary Dax Fell]] produces a ratified [[The Kelrath Envoys|Kelrath]] Council resolution: Hartstone's neutrality supersedes any historical treaty claim. It is dated six months ago. He has been planning this longer than that.

npcs.name: Brother Edmar
npcs.role: Grace-Sworn — chaplain and keeper of founding records
npcs.description: Old enough to remember the last succession crisis. Gentle enough that people tell him things they should not. He spends his evenings in [[The City Archive|the archive]]. He knows something about [[The Underbrick]] he is deciding whether to share.

log.note: [[Brother Edmar]] locates the succession clause in the founding compact: [[The Grace-Sworn]] must recognize the legitimate ruler of any treaty partner. Both [[House Vespar]] claimants have documentation. One set is forged. He does not yet know which.

Consequence of Commander Aldric Vane (ally): [[Commander Aldric Vane]] invokes the armed envoy clause: both delegations must reduce guards to twelve within forty-eight hours. [[Lady Ivra Malt]] protests. [[Emissary Dax Fell]] immediately complies and offers his excess guards to help patrol the tunnels.

npcs.name: Petra Wynn
npcs.role: City archivist — custodian of three generations of records
npcs.description: The city's official archivist. She has copies of everything. She does not sell information — she trades it. She knows who owns the old tunnels under the east quarter and what has moved through [[The East Quarter Tunnels|them]] recently. (DM-only until approached)

npcs.name: The Loom
npcs.description: [[The Underbrick]]'s controller. Nobody inside the network has seen their face. They communicate through cutouts and coded drops. They have been buying weapons from both [[House Vespar|Vespar]] and [[The Kelrath Envoys|Kelrath]] suppliers simultaneously. Either they are playing both sides or they are the third side. (DM-only)

log.note: Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (ally): [[House Vespar]]'s delegation arrives at Hartstone's north gate with forty armed guards. [[Lady Ivra Malt]] presents papers claiming historical title. Impeccably polite, completely immovable.

Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (ally): [[Lady Ivra Malt]] presents her second claimant's documentation at a formal hearing: Lord Eddan Vespar, third cousin, with a direct treaty right to Hartstone under the Compact of 1187. The records are genuine. [[Emissary Dax Fell]] has a counter.

A cutout is captured in the market. Under questioning: [[The Underbrick]] is being paid by two clients simultaneously for the same weapon delivery. [[The Loom]] has sold the same shipment twice. Someone is about to be very angry.
